prompts.label: Hello World iostream
prompts.text:
First C++17 program for a beginner.

- main.cpp with #include <iostream> and std::cout
- Compile: g++ -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic main.cpp -o app
- Explain namespaces std:: and why using namespace std; is discouraged in headers

Contrast one line with C printf.

prompts.label: References and const
prompts.text:
Teach C++ references vs pointers on a swap and print function.

- void swap(int& a, int& b)
- const std::string& for read-only string param
- When references must be bound vs pointers can be null

Small examples only.

prompts.label: std::vector and std::string
prompts.text:
Use STL instead of manual arrays for learning.

- std::vector<int> growth with push_back
- std::string concatenation and size()
- Range-based for loop with auto
- Avoid C-style char* strings in new code habit

Read integers from std::cin into vector until EOF.

prompts.label: Class constructor destructor
prompts.text:
Create class BankAccount with deposit, withdraw, and invariant balance >= 0.

- Constructor, destructor (even if = default explain why)
- private members, public interface
- RAII: resource acquired in constructor released in destructor

No raw new/delete in this exercise.

prompts.label: unique_ptr and make_unique
prompts.text:
Modern C++ memory: std::unique_ptr.

- auto p = std::make_unique<Foo>(args);
- Why unique_ptr prevents leaks on exceptions
- Move ownership with std::move
- Rule: prefer smart pointers over new/delete

Tiny factory function returning unique_ptr.

prompts.label: shared_ptr when sharing
prompts.text:
Explain std::shared_ptr and when multiple owners exist.

- make_shared usage
- Mention weak_ptr breaking cycles — one sentence teaser
- Performance cost vs unique_ptr honest note

Graph node example with shared ownership.

prompts.label: Function templates
prompts.text:
Write a template function min(T a, T b) and use with int and double.

- Template deduction basics
- Why templates live in headers often
- Mention concepts C++20 as later improvement one line

Include explicit instantiation note briefly.

prompts.label: Inheritance and virtual
prompts.text:
Polymorphism with abstract base class Shape and virtual area() = 0.

- override, virtual destructor = default
- std::vector<std::unique_ptr<Shape>>
- When composition over inheritance is better — one paragraph

Circle and Rectangle implementations.

prompts.label: Exceptions try catch
prompts.text:
Use exceptions for exceptional paths in C++.

- throw std::runtime_error with message
- try/catch by const std::exception&
- noexcept when function promises not to throw — intro only
- Do not use exceptions for normal control flow

Parse int with validation example.

prompts.label: Move semantics intro
prompts.text:
Explain lvalues, rvalues, and std::move at beginner depth.

- Move constructor on a simple Buffer class holding int*
- Rule of five names listed (not full impl all)
- Why std::vector push_back is efficient after move

Avoid perfect forwarding deep dive.

prompts.label: std::optional and variant
prompts.text:
Replace magic sentinel values with std::optional.

- parse_int returning optional<int>
- std::variant<string, int> for simple union-like state
- std::visit pattern sketch

When to prefer over exceptions.

prompts.label: enum class scoped enums
prompts.text:
Use enum class instead of plain enum.

- enum class Status { Ok, Error };
- underlying type specification : uint8_t
- switch with static_cast if needed

Compare to C enum pollution problem.

prompts.label: Lambdas and algorithms
prompts.text:
Combine <algorithm> with lambdas.

- std::sort with lambda comparator
- std::find_if on vector
- Capture [=], [&], and cautions on dangling references

Filter even numbers example.

prompts.label: CMake hello project
prompts.text:
Minimal CMake project for C++17.

- cmake_minimum_required, project, add_executable
- set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
- Build steps: cmake -B build && cmake --build build

One library target + main linking it optional.

prompts.label: Operator overloading
prompts.text:
2D vector class with operator+ and operator<< for ostream.

- Friend function for << if appropriate
- Keep operators meaningful — don't overdo
- Explicit constructor for single-arg to avoid surprises

Small demo in main.

prompts.label: std::filesystem paths
prompts.text:
List files in directory with std::filesystem.

- fs::directory_iterator, path extension check
- exists, is_regular_file
- Error code vs exceptions for filesystem ops

Cross-platform path note for Windows.

prompts.label: Header guards modules teaser
prompts.text:
Organize .hpp/.cpp with #pragma once, include what you use.

- Forward declarations to speed compile
- inline functions in headers
- C++20 modules as future direction — 2 sentences only

Split class declaration/definition across files.

prompts.label: GoogleTest or Catch2 unit test
prompts.text:
Add one test framework to a small CMake project.

- Test factorial or add function
- Run ctest after build
- Arrange-act-assert style

Pick Catch2 single-header or GTest — one path only.

prompts.label: const correctness deep dive
prompts.text:
Explain const member functions and const objects.

- int getSize() const;
- const iterator vs iterator on containers
- mutable keyword one-line use case

Fix const-correctness on a broken class sketch.

prompts.label: When to choose C++
prompts.text:
Guide: when C++ is the right tool vs Rust, C, C#, Python.

- Game engines, browsers, trading, existing C++ codebases
- Compile times and complexity honest cons
- Suggested learning order after C++ basics

Bullet list, no code.
